_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Nerve Repair Surgery, Exploration and Ulnar nerve Repair_Medco.pdf
Nerve Repair Surgery,Exploration and Ulnar nerve Repair_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Continuous leg pain (if the pathology is not severe)

preauth.label: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission
X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right)

claim.label: Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
MRI / EMG/ NCV of the affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - confirming the diagnosis submitted?
X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) submitted?
Radiological investigations confirming the need of surgery; +/- NCV report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the Post procedure clinical photographs submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission are missing or incomplete.
X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/Right) is missing or incomplete, or incorrectly labelled.
The clinical notes and imaging reports submitted are not indicative of the procedure.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) are missing or incomplete.
Detailed procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge is missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Performed for continuous leg pain, if the pathology is not severe then the nerve root block has a very good outcome.
Moderate stenosis, facile cyst causing unilateral radiculopathy.
A nerve root block (or a facet block) is the placement of a needle and injection of a combination of local anesthetic and steroid into the sheath surrounding a nerve root in the spine.

A nerve root block may be Diagnostic (Discography) or Diagnostic & therapeutic (Radicular pain (Epidural steroid injection-Transforaminal inter laminar caudal) and Axial pain (Facet joint injection)).
Local anesthetics (Diagnostic par block), Steroids (Epidural steroid injection), Dye (Discography) are the assorted injections usually used in Nerve root procedure based on the objective.
In recent years, ultrasound (US)-guided nerve blocks have gained attention as it offers several advantages than those performed with fluoroscopy or CT.
